rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARY HA PHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/6/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Please provide a courtesy copy of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/23/26. (The petition and accounting have not been fully reviewed yet, as no courtesy copy was provided.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The successor conservator was appointed on 4/16/2009, not 2019.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARL JONE CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The decedent died on 5/12/22. If the heirs do not waive an accounting by the administrator, the first two-year accounting is overdue. [LRSF 14.34 A.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/20/26. Since the continuance is beyond the three months of additional time the administrator requested in this petition to administer the estate, it is assumed that a petition for final distribution will be filed before this continuance date, in which case, this 12200 petition can be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT HOLT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. Proposed Order caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution" 2. Proof of satisfaction for American Express claim has not been filed. 3. The attorney of record or the Court-appointed fiduciary must E-File a declaration certifying that the original bank statement will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257 [Local Rule 14.59A]. 4. The decedent's will directs that a trust be established for the care of her pets. Please explain and provide a memo of points and authorities why the caretaker's current refusal to accept such funds overrides that directive. 5. The Power of Attorney that authorized Jordan Holt to assign Anne Lewis' interest in this estate must be filed. 6. Waivers of Account have not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF HAYDEN AINSLEY ARTHUR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: **REPOSTED 11/14/25 at 10:20 a.m.** Continued to 1/26/2026. Required documents must be filed two weeks prior to the next hearing. Please see the order [GC-240] dated on 8/5/2025. (1) Receipts for the funds placed in a blocked account along with a request to waive accountings (2) Proof of Payment of Assessment Fee [PRB-PCN-004] $800 (3) Inventory and Appraisal [DE-160/GC-040] (4) Proof of mailing of Notice of Filing Inventory and Appraisal and How to Object to the Inventory or Appraised Value of the Property [GC-240] PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOLITA C. ARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no notice of a supplement was necessary, but if notice must be given in a probate matter, the mandatory Judicial Council form must be used, and mailing notice on 11/12/25 for a hearing on 11/17/25 was not valid notice in any event (not 15 days' notice for a petition for letters). Status of closing the estate will be set for 5/17/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 5/17/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLEVELAND GALLAT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Letters issued to the personal representative of the estate Michael Gallat, III, must be filed. (2) Letters issued to the personal representative of the estate of his reportedly now deceased sole beneficiary, Michael Wayne Chase, must also be filed. (3) Patricia D. Chase's address was not set forth in the verified supplement. (4) If she does, in fact, have standing as the personal representative of this decedent's sole heir-at-law and also of the sole beneficiary of this decedent's sole heir-at-law, has she consented in writing to have notice mailed to her attorney alone, not also to her? [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] (5) Who is Robin A. Lawler (a deceased person) to whom notice was given also in care of another? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/3/25. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A copy of the petition does not need to be attached to the notice. Examiners are not interested parties entitled to notice.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JINGLU WU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Limited Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Non-residents of the United States are ineligible for appointment as personal representative (Probate Code Section 8402(a)(4). 2) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States (Probate Code Section 8113). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATILDA A. ANDRES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice showed that an AMENDED petition is to be heard. (2) When the whereabouts of an interested party are unknown, an ex parte petition must be filed to dispense with notice to them. The ex parte petition must set forth the efforts made to locate them. (3) If a decedent is not survived by any known next of kin, the petitioner must file a statement, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.2] Who were the decedent's spouse, his parents, and his siblings who were the parents of his niblings, and when did they die? Are the beneficiaries all of the heirs of the decedent's predeceased spouse? (5) It is not entirely clear that the will is self-proving. None of the witnesses swore under penalty of perjury that they signed the will, and the purported notary merely acknowledged the signatures but she did not also sign the acknowledgment (and an acknowledgement is not a jurat). Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/23/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
